file-as-state.rule: File has content → previously started → ask user where to resume
File exists but empty → not started → begin from step 1
File does not exist → not planned → suggest running upstream workflow
NEVER use state markers, frontmatter status, or external state files. All progress is determined by reading actual file content.
When resuming, read the file content, identify completed sections by their headings, and ask the user which section to continue from.

read-write-boundaries.rule: JS action scripts only perform initialization and read-only operations. They NEVER modify existing file content.
All content writing and modification is done by the LLM directly (via tool calls).
Never overwrite existing content without explicit user confirmation.
Directory creation (mkdir -p) and empty file creation (touch) are done by the LLM via shell commands.
Files in interview/*/source/ are read-only after being copied by problem-copy.js.

teaching-menu.option: Deep dive — go deeper on current topic
Write — distill current dialogue into document

interview-menu.option: Hint — give me a hint (do NOT give the answer)
Finished — I am done with this part

menu-rules.rule: Always display the relevant menu after completing a section or action.
Wait for user input before proceeding. Never auto-advance.
Menu keys are case-insensitive. Accept both 'c' and 'C'.

write-behavior.rule: [W] is available at any point during teaching and interview workflows.
Write target depends on context: - During lesson-teach → lesson.md (teaching essence) or practice.md (exercises) - During interview-run → interview.md (interview record and evaluation)
Written content is a high-quality distillation of the dialogue, NOT a conversation transcript. Organize by topic, use clear headings, include key insights and examples.
After writing, return to the current workflow step and redisplay the menu.
Append new content to existing file content. Never replace what was previously written unless user explicitly asks.

language-rules.rule: Code examples and comments follow the document output language.

context-update.rule: During any workflow, when you discover important learner information (weak areas, strengths, preferences, background changes, learning breakthroughs), proactively prompt: "This seems important — update your learning profile?"
Only update docs/progress/learning-context.md after user confirms.
Append new observations. Preserve and refine existing entries.

workflow-execution.rule: Every workflow MUST follow this initialization sequence: 1. Read and internalize this engine.xml 2. Read .masterclass/config/config.yaml (or use config-read.js output) 3. Read docs/progress/learning-context.md 4. Execute workflow.md instructions
Execute steps in strict sequential order. Never skip steps. Never load future steps prematurely.
Read one step file fully, execute it completely, then move to the next.
Never generate content without user input. This is collaborative facilitation.
Always get user confirmation before writing files or creating structure.
